conclusion_summary: Fresh-state nsys: 116 GFLOP/s vs benchmark 42 GFLOP/s (2.76x thermal ratio). True efficiency ~0.66 after thermal correction. Root cause dual: P004 API Limitation (RAJA forall cannot express shared-memory tiling) + P004 Thermal Contamination (raja_naive runs after ~130s of native+cublas GPU load in sequential session; 1200ms/iter x 50 warmup = 60s differential vs native 785ms/iter x 50 = 39s). Benchmark eff=0.24 is not representative of abstraction ceiling.

Fresh-state nsys: ~225 GFLOP/s vs benchmark 139 GFLOP/s (1.62x thermal ratio). True efficiency ~1.29 at medium N — Pattern 5 (Layout-Induced Coalescing Advantage) is size-independent, not N-dependent as initially hypothesized. Non-monotonic scaling (0.80 medium -> 1.25 large) was entirely a thermal artifact. Julia large is consistent: profiling 211 vs benchmark 220 GFLOP/s (4% delta, no thermal artifact at large N because julia_naive runs last in session after GPU has re-stabilized post-raja_naive).

Measure @cuda kernel dispatch time vs compute time using CUDA events. Hypothesis: CUDA.synchronize() + @cuda overhead is ~0.03-0.05ms, competing with kernel time ~0.05ms at N=1024. ncu target: sm__cycles_elapsed, l1tex__t_requests, launch overhead via --set=full. Expected: confirm P001 accounts for ~44% of wall time at this problem size.

Measure L2 hit rate under random column access pattern. ncu metrics: l2_read_hit_rate, dram_read_transactions, l2_read_sector_queries. Hypothesis: random_sparse columns (uniform sample over N=32768) generate ~163K unique 64-byte cache lines per rep — exceeds L2 capacity; Kokkos adds ~20% overhead vs RAJA (0.92) vs native (1.0 reference). Expected: L2 hit rate < 30% vs laplacian_2d L2 hit rate > 60%.

Measure warp occupancy and thread utilisation under Pareto gamma=2.5 degree distribution. ncu metrics: sm__warps_active.avg.pct_of_peak_sustained_active, thread_inst_executed, inst_executed. Hypothesis: eff=0.45 = P001 factor (0.56/1.0) × P007 factor (0.45/0.56 = 0.80). Most warps stall on max-degree rows (up to 256 nnz) while minimum-degree rows (1 nnz) complete in 1 cycle. Expected: warp occupancy < 40% at N=1024 power_law.

ncu SM occupancy analysis for random_sparse/small. All abstractions beat native at N=1024 (kokkos=1.30, raja=1.61, julia=1.30 vs native=1.0). Hypothesis: native kernel launch path has higher constant overhead (cudaLaunchKernel via C API) at this problem size; abstractions may use stream-ordered launches or pre-compiled kernel objects with lower dispatch latency. Alternative: SM under-occupancy at N=1024 means 5 warps total — any difference in launch timing shows up as apparent throughput difference. ncu target: l1tex__average_t_request_input_sector_queries_mem_global.ratio, sm__warps_active.
